items.title: Wrote about the big NBA thing that happened today: the latest hearing for Terry Rozier in federal court and a request from a federal judge for the NBA to advise whether they want him to be able to play in the NBA.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt:
Wrote about the big NBA thing that happened today: the latest hearing for Terry Rozier in federal court and a request from a federal judge for the NBA to advise whether they want him to be able to play in the NBA.

Free to read for all: www.nytimes.com/athletic/734...

media.title: Terry Rozier’s trial date set for 2027 as NBA future grows uncertain
media.description: Rozier has pleaded not guilty to all four charges. The judge set a Feb. 8, 2027 date for trial.

items.title: Terry Rozier pleads not guilty to new charges; Feb. trial set
items.author: ESPN
items.body_excerpt: Terry Rozier pleads not guilty to new charges; Feb. trial set ESPN

items.title: Rozier's lawyer has asked the judge to lift a bond restriction that effectively keeps him from playing in the NBA. Judge has asked the NBA to weigh in by Monday. The trial date for the case has been set for Feb. 8, 2027. Rozier has asked the judge to dismiss the charges.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt: Rozier's lawyer has asked the judge to lift a bond restriction that effectively keeps him from playing in the NBA. Judge has asked the NBA to weigh in by Monday. The trial date for the case has been set for Feb. 8, 2027. Rozier has asked the judge to dismiss the charges.

items.title: Jim Trusty, lawyer for Terry Rozier: "The NBA has literally flagrantly fouled the presumption of innocence...They're still trying to find ways to pretend they're victims & that Terry should be prohibited from playing basketball... They are not on the side of angels in this case."
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt: Jim Trusty, lawyer for Terry Rozier: "The NBA has literally flagrantly fouled the presumption of innocence...They're still trying to find ways to pretend they're victims & that Terry should be prohibited from playing basketball... They are not on the side of angels in this case."

media.description: Papers filed Wednesday show that the former Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier will not be receiving the majority of his salary, but has regained the right to again interact with the Heat.

items.title: Rozier's lawyers say his current bond release conditions keep him from playing in the NBA and have asked a federal judge to change them.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt:
Rozier's lawyers say his current bond release conditions keep him from playing in the NBA and have asked a federal judge to change them.

“With forced inactivity, Mr. Rozier is facing a potential second season of non-participation despite being entitled to a presumption of innocence," they wrote.

items.title: [Vorkunov] Terry Rozier was in violation of his NBA contract and will forfeit most of his $26.6 million salary for the 2025-26 season. A lawyer for Rozier asked the judge overseeing his case to change the conditions of his release because Rozier still hopes to play in the NBA.

items.title: Terry Rozier won't get paid much of his $26.6 million salary this season after an arbitrator ruled for the NBA.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt:
Terry Rozier won't get paid much of his $26.6 million salary this season after an arbitrator ruled for the NBA. 

And he still wants to play in the NBA. Wednesday, his lawyers asked a judge to change his terms of release so he could try.

Free to read www.nytimes.com/athletic/733...

media.title: Terry Rozier to forfeit most of $26.6 million salary, arbitrator rules (Gift Article)
media.description: The move comes after Rozier was in violation of his NBA contract this season due to his alleged role in a sports gambling scheme.

items.title: An arbitrator ruled in favor of the NBA and against Terry Rozier, saying the conditions of his release, which prevent contact with the Heat and imposed travel restrictions, made him in violation of his NBA contract. So Rozier will not be paid most of his $26.6 million salary for
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt: An arbitrator ruled in favor of the NBA and against Terry Rozier, saying the conditions of his release, which prevent contact with the Heat and imposed travel restrictions, made him in violation of his NBA contract. So Rozier will not be paid most of his $26.6 million salary for this season.

items.title: Federal prosecutors filed new charges against NBA veteran Terry Rozier today. Prosecutors say Rozier took a roughly $100,000 "bribe" to exit early from a March 2023 Hornets game so a group of sports gamblers could win bets on his props.
items.author: NBA Stat
items.body_excerpt:
Federal prosecutors filed new charges against NBA veteran Terry Rozier today. Prosecutors say Rozier took a roughly $100,000 "bribe" to exit early from a March 2023 Hornets game so a group of sports gamblers could win bets on his props.

The game in question...

(via Mike Vorkunov)
#NBA

items.title: A lawyer for Rozier, Jim Trusty, said: “The new indictment confirms that our motion to dismiss was a good one — it’s just new charges and new theories trotted out in the hope that something sticks.” Rozier has been charged with sports bribery and honest services fraud.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt: A lawyer for Rozier, Jim Trusty, said: “The new indictment confirms that our motion to dismiss was a good one — it’s just new charges and new theories trotted out in the hope that something sticks.” Rozier has been charged with sports bribery and honest services fraud.

items.title: A defendant in the federal NBA gambling case told a judge today he paid an NBA player to change his performance so he could bet on him. The player, a federal prosecutor said, was former Charlotte Hornets and Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt:
A defendant in the federal NBA gambling case told a judge today he paid an NBA player to change his performance so he could bet on him. The player, a federal prosecutor said, was former Charlotte Hornets and Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier. 

Free to read👇 www.nytimes.com/athletic/731...

media.title: Marves Fairley claims he paid Terry Rozier as part of illegal gambling scheme (Gift Article)
media.description: Fairley pleaded guilty Thursday to seven felony counts against two separate cases brought by the Department of Justice over the last year.
